register_notice: Internal only — never linked from the public dashboard. See dfapti/rules/factualism-audit.md, 'Target Visibility Gate'. A candidate is promoted to a public case only once at least one source_evidence entry is independently verified to the same bar as an ACCEPTED evidence record.

candidates.name: Canva Pty Ltd

source_evidence.title: 26-090MR Canva Group pays $792,000 in infringement notices for failing to lodge financial reports on time

candidates.public_interest_reason: Named in an ASIC media release as one of four Canva Group entities, paying an infringement notice for allegedly failing to lodge an audited financial report by the statutory deadline — a corporate financial-transparency compliance matter.
risk_indicators: Received an ASIC infringement notice ($198,000) for alleged late lodgement of an audited financial report, per 26-090MR

candidates.name: Liberty Primary Metals Australia Pty Ltd

source_evidence.title: 25-141MR Three GFG Alliance Group companies ordered to lodge outstanding annual reports with ASIC

candidates.public_interest_reason: Named in ASIC media release 25-141MR (and the preceding 25-113MR) as a GFG Alliance Group company subject to Supreme Court of NSW orders over alleged failure to lodge annual financial reports.
risk_indicators: Subject of Supreme Court of NSW civil proceedings (commenced June 2025) and consent orders (21 August 2025) over alleged failure to lodge its FY2024 annual report, per 25-141MR/25-113MR
Reported to be in voluntary administration as of a subsequent ASIC published notice

candidates.name: Liberty Bell Bay Pty Ltd

source_evidence.title: 25-141MR Three GFG Alliance Group companies ordered to lodge outstanding annual reports with ASIC
26-041MR ASIC files Court action to wind up Liberty Bell Bay after failures to lodge financial reports

candidates.public_interest_reason: Named in ASIC media release 25-141MR (and the preceding 25-113MR) as a GFG Alliance Group company subject to Supreme Court of NSW orders over alleged failure to lodge annual financial reports across multiple years; escalated to a winding-up application per 26-041MR.
risk_indicators: Subject of Supreme Court of NSW civil proceedings over alleged failure to lodge annual reports for FY2021-FY2024, per 25-141MR/25-113MR
Reported to have failed to comply with the resulting court orders, leading ASIC to file a winding-up application (26-041MR)

candidates.name: JustAnswer LLC

source_evidence.title: JustAnswer in Court for alleged misleading prices, subscription trap and government affiliation claims

candidates.public_interest_reason: Subject of Federal Court proceedings brought by the ACCC (ACCC v JustAnswer LLC, VID1259 of 2025), ordered to pay $10 million in penalties for allegedly misleading consumers about subscription pricing (a '$2' offer that led to undisclosed ongoing charges of $50-90/month) and falsely implying affiliation with the Fair Work Ombudsman.
risk_indicators: Ordered by the Federal Court to pay $10 million in penalties in ACCC v JustAnswer LLC (VID1259 of 2025)
ACCC alleged misleading pricing representations and false government-affiliation claims

candidates.name: Vacaciones eDreams, S.L

source_evidence.title: Online travel agency eDreams pays penalties and gives undertaking over allegedly misleading subscription prices

candidates.public_interest_reason: Paid $59,400 in ACCC infringement notices and gave a court-enforceable s.87B undertaking over allegedly misleading subscription pricing practices (advertising a subscription as '$0.00'/'FREE' without adequately disclosing conditions, and not prominently displaying total annual cost).
risk_indicators: Paid $59,400 in ACCC infringement notices for allegedly misleading subscription pricing claims
Gave the ACCC a court-enforceable undertaking (s.87B) admitting conduct contravened or likely contravened the Australian Consumer Law

candidates.name: Christofi Investments

source_evidence.title: Ugg boot retailer pays penalties for strikethrough pricing claims

candidates.public_interest_reason: Paid $39,600 in ACCC infringement notices for allegedly misleading 'strikethrough' pricing claims on ugg-boot retail websites it owns/administers (Original Ugg Australia Classic, Ugg Originals).
risk_indicators: Paid $39,600 in ACCC infringement notices for allegedly misleading strikethrough pricing claims
